violations.explanation: The page_additions file defines locators as standalone module-level variables instead of inside an Elements inner class. The framework pattern requires locators to be defined inside a class Elements: block within the page object. The merge instructions say 'ADD TO EXISTING PrintHistoryPage.Elements CLASS' but the actual code shows standalone variables. This violates the Page Object Model pattern where locators must be in an Elements container class.

fix_details.description: Wrap all locator definitions in a class Elements: block
fix_details.required_structure:
class Elements:
    plan_pause_info = "[data-testid='plan-pause-info']"
    billing_cycle_period_card = "[data-testid='billing-cycle-period-card']"
    # ... all other locators

This is a secondary issue dependent on V065.

fix_priority_order.fix_approach: Replace all page.locator(print_history_page.elements.X) with direct property access print_history_page.X. First, add property definitions to page_additions file in __init__ method: self.X = page.locator(self.elements.X) for each locator. Then replace all inline page.locator() calls in test script with direct property access. This is the highest priority fix as it accounts for 96 points of deduction.

Complete all precondition setup implementations. Remove TODO comments and implement actual methods: 1) Add plan selection method call for 50 pages plan, 2) Remove misleading TODO comment about subscription ID (code is already implemented), 3) Implement subscription pause method via GeminiRAHelper, 4) Add logging for free months verification. These are critical because incomplete preconditions mean the test cannot run correctly.
